topic: VS Code Goes Weekly: The New Era of Rapid Feature Releases
hook: VS Code just went weekly — here's what that means for your workflow
audience: VS Code power users and developers who want to stay on top of the latest features
keyTakeaway: Weekly stable releases mean faster feature delivery but require new habits for staying current — here's how to adapt
talkingPoints: VS Code 1.111 marks the first weekly Stable release — a major shift from monthly cadence
Why this matters: AI features are evolving so fast that monthly releases couldn't keep up
The engineering changes behind this: automated testing, PR media attachment, showcase pipelines
What's deprecated: Edit Mode is officially deprecated, fully removed by version 1.125
My strategy: How I'm adapting my content calendar to cover weekly VS Code drops
trendContext: VS Code 1.111 (March 9, 2026) explicitly announces 'the first of our weekly Stable releases' - this is a fundamental shift in how VS Code ships features, driven by the pace of AI development
